cards.title: Life Cycle Approach
cards.content:
ΓÇó Young Unmarried: Low insurance need, high risk appetite.
ΓÇó Married with Kids: Highest need for life insurance (protection).
ΓÇó Retired: Focus shifts from protection to regular income (Pension/Annuities).

cards.title: Risk & Return
cards.content:
ΓÇó There is always an inverse relationship between Risk and Return. Higher the risk, higher the potential return (e.g., Equity).
ΓÇó Insurance provides low return but guarantees absolute safety and protection.

items.text: Earning interest on interest. The earlier you start saving, the greater the compounding effect.
Time Value of Money: Money today is worth more than the same amount in the future due to inflation.
Section 80C allows premium deductions. Section 10(10D) makes maturity proceeds tax-free.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Financial planning is the process of meeting life goals through proper management of finances.
Savings vs Insurance: Savings cannot suddenly provide a huge amount in an emergency; Insurance immediately pays the full Sum Assured.
Section 80D is specifically for Health Insurance premium tax deductions.
An ideal investment portfolio should be a mix of Liquid, Safe, and Growth assets.
The primary purpose of Life Insurance is Protection, not investment.
